guideline_summary: Implement Zero Trust Architecture: never trust, always verify. Authenticate and authorize every request at every service boundary.
text: Implement Zero Trust Architecture: treat all network traffic as untrusted. Authenticate and authorize every request at every service boundary using JWT or mTLS. Do not rely on network perimeter security alone. Implement service-to-service authentication via service accounts and short-lived credentials.

guideline_summary: Encrypt all data in transit using TLS 1.3. Encrypt sensitive data at rest using AES-256. Manage encryption keys with a dedicated KMS.
text: Encrypt all data in transit using TLS 1.3 minimum. Encrypt sensitive data at rest using AES-256. Never store encryption keys alongside encrypted data. Use a dedicated Key Management Service (AWS KMS, Google Cloud KMS, HashiCorp Vault) for key rotation and access control. Rotate keys at least annually.

guideline_summary: Use OAuth 2.0 with PKCE for public clients. Use client credentials flow for service-to-service communication. Implement short-lived tokens (15 minute access tokens).
text: Use OAuth 2.0 with PKCE for public clients (SPAs, mobile apps). Use client credentials flow for service-to-service communication. Issue short-lived access tokens (15 minutes) with refresh tokens. Validate tokens at the API gateway layer. Implement token revocation using a blocklist or opaque tokens.

guideline_summary: Implement Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) or Attribute-Based Access Control (ABAC). Apply least-privilege principle to all service accounts.
text: Implement Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) or Attribute-Based Access Control (ABAC). Grant minimum permissions required (least-privilege principle). Review and audit access entitlements quarterly. Use service accounts with minimal scopes for automated processes. Log all authorization decisions.

guideline_summary: Validate and sanitize all input. Use parameterized queries to prevent SQL injection. Implement input length limits and content-type validation.
text: Validate and sanitize all user input at the API boundary. Use parameterized queries or prepared statements to prevent SQL injection. Implement input length limits, regex validation, and content-type validation. Use a WAF (Web Application Firewall) to block common OWASP Top 10 attack vectors.

guideline_summary: Never log sensitive data: passwords, credit cards, PII, tokens. Implement log scrubbing. Use structured logging with severity levels.
text: Never log sensitive data including passwords, credit cards, PII (personally identifiable information), API keys, or tokens. Implement log scrubbing/masking in the logging pipeline. Use structured JSON logging with severity levels (DEBUG, INFO, WARN, ERROR). Ship logs to a centralized SIEM with access controls.

guideline_summary: Implement API rate limiting and throttling to prevent DoS attacks. Use distributed rate limiting with Redis for multi-instance deployments.
text: Implement API rate limiting and throttling at the gateway layer. Use token bucket or sliding window algorithm. For multi-instance deployments, use distributed rate limiting with Redis. Return 429 Too Many Requests with Retry-After header. Apply different limits per client type (authenticated vs anonymous, partner vs public).

guideline_summary: Implement secrets rotation for all credentials. Use short-lived credentials from IAM roles rather than long-lived API keys where possible.
text: Implement automated secrets rotation for all credentials (database passwords, API keys, certificates). Use IAM roles and instance profiles for cloud service authentication instead of long-lived API keys. Rotate TLS certificates before expiry using automated tools (Let's Encrypt, AWS Certificate Manager). Alert on secrets approaching expiry.

guideline_summary: Perform threat modeling (STRIDE) for all new services. Conduct SAST, DAST, and dependency vulnerability scanning in the CI/CD pipeline.
text: Perform threat modeling using STRIDE methodology for all new services. Integrate security scanning into CI/CD: SAST (static analysis), DAST (dynamic testing), SCA (dependency vulnerability scanning), and container image scanning. Block deployments with critical CVEs. Conduct penetration tests quarterly.

guideline_summary: Implement CORS properly. Allow only specific trusted origins. Never use wildcard (*) CORS in production APIs.
text: Implement CORS (Cross-Origin Resource Sharing) policies correctly. Allow only specific trusted origins, not a wildcard (*). Validate the Origin header server-side. Include SameSite=Strict on cookies. Implement Content Security Policy (CSP) headers to prevent XSS. Use HSTS (HTTP Strict Transport Security) to enforce HTTPS.
